Bash(GEMINI_API_KEY="AIzaSyBkJL4OmqeMu5BlZ03fuSNMLHf_TyGOwkc" RAW_CONTENT="Here are today's top AI news stories:

1. OpenAI releases GPT-5 with native tool use and 1M context window
OpenAI announced GPT-5, their most capable model yet. It features native tool use \(the model can call APIs, run code, and browse the web without plugins\), a 1 million token context window, and significantly improved reasoning. Available today via API. Source: https://openai.com/blog/gpt5 \(openai.com\)

2. Google DeepMind achieves breakthrough in protein-drug interaction prediction
DeepMind's new AlphaFold 3.5 can predict how drugs interact with proteins with 95% accuracy, potentially cutting drug discovery timelines from years to months. The model is open-source. Source: https://deepmind.google/alphafold35 \(deepmind.google\)

3. EU passes AI Liability Directive requiring explainability for all AI decisions affecting consumers
The European Union finalized the AI Liability Directive, making companies legally liable for AI-driven decisions that harm consumers. Companies must provide explanations for automated decisions upon request. Enforcement begins January 2027. Source: https://ec.europa.eu/ai-liability \(ec.europa.eu\)

4. Nvidia announces H200 Ultra GPU with 288GB HBM4 memory
Nvidia revealed the H200 Ultra, featuring 288GB of next-gen HBM4 memory and 2x the inference throughput of H100. Shipping Q3 2026. Source: https://nvidia.com/h200ultra \(nvidia.com\)

5. Anthropic launches Claude Agent SDK for building autonomous AI workflows
Anthropic released an open-source SDK for building multi-step AI agent workflows with Claude. It includes built-in tool use, memory management, and orchestration patterns. Source: https://anthropic.com/agent-sdk \(anthropic.com\)

6. Small startup releases yet another AI writing assistant
A startup called WriteBot launched an AI writing tool. It uses GPT-4 under the hood and offers grammar checking and tone adjustment. Source: https://writebot.ai \(writebot.ai\)

7. Samsung adds AI photo editing to Galaxy S27
Samsung announced AI-powered photo editing features in their upcoming Galaxy S27 phone, including object removal and style transfer. Source: https://samsung.com/galaxy-s27 \(samsung.com\)

8. Meta open-sources Llama 4 with 400B parameters
Meta released Llama 4, a 400B parameter open-source model that rivals GPT-4 on most benchmarks. Available for commercial use under a permissive license. Source: https://ai.meta.com/llama4 \(ai.meta.com\)" node scripts/process-digest.mjs 2>&1)
